name: getsploit
title: getsploit

commands.name: getsploit

arguments.description: Mirror (aka copies) exploits to the current
arguments.raw: -m, --mirror               Mirror (aka copies) exploits to the current

arguments.description: Perform search in the local database instead of
arguments.raw: -l, --local                Perform search in the local database instead of

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# getsploit --help
 Usage: getsploit [OPTIONS] [QUERY]...
 
 Exploit search and download utility
 
 Options:
 -j, --json                 Show result in JSON format
 -m, --mirror               Mirror (aka copies) exploits to the current
                            working directory
 -l, --local                Perform search in the local database instead of
                            searching online
 -u, --update               Update getsploit.db database
 -s, --set-key              Set vulners API key
 -k, --api-key TEXT         Vulners API key
 -c, --count INTEGER RANGE  Search limit  [1<=x<=1000]
 --help                     Show this message and exit.

local_help.help: Error: Vulners API key not found.

apt_metadata.description:
Command line utility for searching and downloading exploits
Inspired by searchsploit, getsploit combines two features: command line search
and download.
.
It allows you to search online for the exploits across all the most popular
collections, including (but not limited to):
- Exploit-DB,
- Metasploit,
- Packetstorm
.
The most powerful feature of getsploit is the ability to immediately download
the exploit source code in your working path.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use getsploit because ### getsploit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `getsploit [OPTIONS] [QUERY]...` as documented upstream for getsploit.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair getsploit with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `getsploit --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install getsploit. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `getsploit [OPTIONS] [QUERY]...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### getsploit.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install getsploit
Install with: sudo apt install getsploit
